ansi.c(3) Library Functions Manual ansi.c(3)

NAME

ansi.c

SYNOPSIS

Functions


char * ansi_strip_escapes (const char *input, size_t input_len)
bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char *message, size_t pos)

Function Documentation

bool ansi_is_already_colorized (const char * message, size_t pos)

Definition at line 64 of file ansi.c.

64                                                                 {
65   if (!message) {
66     return false;
67   }
68 
69   bool in_reset = true; // Start in reset state
70 
71   // Scan from beginning to current position looking for ANSI codes
72   for (size_t i = 0; i < pos && message[i] != '\0'; i++) {
73     if (message[i] == '\x1b' && message[i + 1] == '[') {
74       // Found ANSI escape sequence start
75       // Look for the end marker 'm'
76       size_t j = i + 2;
77       while (message[j] != '\0' && message[j] != 'm') {
78         j++;
79       }
80 
81       if (message[j] == 'm') {
82         // Extract the color code part (between [ and m)
83         // Check if it's a reset code: \x1b[0m or \x1b[m
84         if ((j == i + 3 && message[i + 2] == '0') || (j == i + 2)) {
85           // Reset code
86           in_reset = true;
87         } else {
88           // Color code (anything else)
89           in_reset = false;
90         }
91       }
92     }
93   }
94 
95   // Return true if NOT in reset state (already colorized)
96   return !in_reset;
97 }




Referenced by colorize_log_message().






char
  * ansi_strip_escapes (const char * input, size_t input_len)


Definition at line 13 of file ansi.c.


13                                                               {
14   if (!input || input_len == 0) {
15     return NULL;
16   }
17 
18   // Allocate buffer directly for output (at most input_len bytes)
19   char *output = SAFE_MALLOC(input_len + 1, char *);
20   if (!output) {
21     return NULL;
22   }
23 
24   size_t out_pos = 0;
25   size_t i = 0;
26 
27   while (i < input_len) {
28     // Check for ESC character (start of ANSI sequence)
29     if (input[i] == '\x1b' && i + 1 < input_len && input[i + 1] == '[') {
30       // Skip ESC[
31       i += 2;
32 
33       // Skip parameter bytes (digits, semicolons, and intermediate bytes)
34       while (i < input_len) {
35         char c = input[i];
36         // Parameter bytes: 0x30-0x3F (digits, semicolon, etc.)
37         // Intermediate bytes: 0x20-0x2F (space, !, ", etc.)
38         if ((c >= 0x30 && c <= 0x3F) || (c >= 0x20 && c <= 0x2F)) {
39           i++;
40         } else {
41           break;
42         }
43       }
44 
45       // Skip final byte (0x40-0x7E: @, A-Z, [, \, ], ^, _, `, a-z, {, |, }, ~)
46       if (i < input_len) {
47         char final = input[i];
48         if (final >= 0x40 && final <= 0x7E) {
49           i++;
50         }
51       }
52     } else {
53       // Regular character - copy to output
54       output[out_pos++] = input[i];
55       i++;
56     }
57   }
58 
59   // Null-terminate the output
60   output[out_pos] = '\0';
61   return output;
62 }

    layout_print_two_column_row(), and log_mmap_write().
